data.narrative:
Chariot Defense is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $41M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 firms—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage, including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and Anduril Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $41M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $82M. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $400M, though the range is wide: $150M (Ares Industries) to $29.0B (Scale AI), reflecting material heterogeneity within the defense tech space.

The triangulated valuation range spans $70M (low) to $460M (high), with a median implied value of $241M. The substantial spread reflects two distinct narratives: stage-based mechanics suggest a floor near $70M–$82M, while peer multiples—anchored by the $400M median—point toward meaningfully higher territory. Key uncertainties include competitive positioning relative to the outlier-weighted peer set and revenue traction relative to capital deployed. Resolution of near-term commercial milestones would likely compress this range materially.
